name: senior-architect
description: Comprehensive software architecture skill for designing scalable, maintainable systems using ReactJS, NextJS, NodeJS, Express, React Native, Swift, Kotlin, Flutter, Postgres, GraphQL, Go, Python. Includes architecture diagram generation, system design patterns, tech stack decision frameworks, and dependency analysis. Use when designing system architecture, making technical decisions, creating architecture diagrams, evaluating trade-offs, or defining integration patterns.
capabilities: Apply proven architecture patterns (microservices, event-driven, layered) to new projects
Design system workflows for scalability, performance, and maintainability
Make informed technology stack decisions based on project requirements
Generate architecture diagrams and project structures automatically
Analyze dependencies and provide optimization recommendations
Implement security best practices in system design
author: sickn33
securitySummary: Security audit completed. Static findings are false positives: external_commands (33) triggered by markdown code blocks, filesystem access is legitimate output functionality, sensitive finding is standard .env setup. No actual security risks identified. Skill is safe for marketplace publication.
valueStatement: Senior developers struggle to design systems that scale and remain maintainable over time. This skill provides architecture patterns, system design workflows, and technical decision frameworks to build enterprise-grade applications.
limitations: Does not write application code - provides architecture guidance only
Scripts are templates that require customization for production use
Does not provision cloud infrastructure or manage deployments
Does not perform runtime security auditing

useCases.targetUser: Technical leads and architects starting new projects
useCases.title: Architect New SaaS Platform
useCases.description: Design the architecture for a new multi-tenant SaaS application including frontend, backend, database, and infrastructure choices

useCases.title: Review Existing System Design
useCases.description: Analyze an existing codebase and provide architecture improvements for scalability and maintainability

useCases.title: Technology Stack Selection
useCases.description: Evaluate and select appropriate technologies for a project based on requirements, team expertise, and long-term maintainability

promptTemplates.title: Basic Architecture Request
promptTemplates.prompt: I need to design a [type of application] that handles [number] users. What architecture patterns would you recommend? Consider [specific requirement].
promptTemplates.scenario: Quick architecture consultation for a new project

promptTemplates.title: Detailed System Design
promptTemplates.prompt: Design a system architecture for a [description of system]. Include: 1) Component diagram 2) Data flow 3) API design 4) Database schema 5) Security considerations. The system must handle [scale requirements].

promptTemplates.title: Technology Selection
promptTemplates.prompt: I am building a [application type] with these requirements: [list requirements]. Compare [Technology A] vs [Technology B] vs [Technology C] for the [component]. Recommend the best choice with rationale.

promptTemplates.title: Architecture Review
promptTemplates.prompt: Review the architecture of my existing [system description]. Identify: 1) Scalability bottlenecks 2) Security vulnerabilities 3) Maintainability issues 4) Performance concerns. Provide specific recommendations for improvement.

bestPractices: Start with requirements before choosing architecture - understand scale, complexity, and team capabilities first
Document architecture decisions with pros, cons, and trade-offs using Architecture Decision Records (ADRs)
Design for failure - plan for component failures and have graceful degradation strategies
antiPatterns: Over-engineering early - do not apply microservices patterns to simple applications that could work as monoliths
Ignoring non-functional requirements - performance, security, and scalability must be considered from the start
Technology hopping - avoid switching tech stacks frequently as it creates technical debt and slows delivery

faq.question: What programming languages and frameworks does this skill support?
faq.answer: The skill covers React, NextJS, NodeJS, Express, React Native, Swift, Kotlin, Flutter, Go, Python, PostgreSQL, GraphQL, and more. It provides architecture guidance for any technology stack.

faq.question: Does this skill write code or just provide guidance?
faq.answer: This skill focuses on architecture design and provides guidance, patterns, and recommendations. The included scripts generate project structures and analyze dependencies, but detailed implementation code is outside scope.

faq.question: Can I use this for existing system refactoring?
faq.answer: Yes, you can describe your current architecture and the skill will analyze it for bottlenecks, security issues, and improvement opportunities.

faq.question: How do the included Python scripts work?
faq.answer: The scripts (architecture_diagram_generator.py, project_architect.py, dependency_analyzer.py) are starter tools. Run them with a project path to generate diagrams and analyze dependencies. Customize them for your specific needs.

faq.question: Is this suitable for beginners?
faq.answer: This skill is designed for senior developers and architects. Beginners should first understand fundamentals of software design, data structures, and web development before diving into architecture.

faq.question: How does this integrate with Claude Code?
faq.answer: This skill extends Claude Code with architecture expertise. You can ask architecture questions in natural language and receive detailed guidance, patterns, and best practices.
